evals.name: eval-webhook-endpoint
evals.prompt: I have an approved design for a DocuSeal webhook endpoint. The design specifies: a new route at POST /webhooks/docuseal that receives signature completion events, validates the HMAC signature, updates the Document model status, and sends a confirmation email to the landlord. The scope is: In Scope: webhook route, HMAC validation, document status update, email notification. Out of Scope: DocuSeal template management, document upload, tenant-facing UI. Please write the implementation plan following Phase 2 of PlanCraft — I need bite-sized TDD tasks with exact file paths and complete code.
evals.expected_output: A plan with: plan document header, file structure map, bite-sized TDD tasks (2-5 min each) with complete code, exact file paths, test commands with expected output, scope section

assertions.text: Plan has a document header with Goal, Architecture, and Tech Stack fields
Plan includes a file structure map (table or list) showing files to create/modify with responsibilities
Tasks follow TDD cycle: write failing test -> verify it fails -> implement -> verify pass -> commit
Code blocks contain complete implementation code, not placeholders like 'add validation here'
Uses real project file paths from the CourierFlow codebase (app/routes/*, app/services/*, etc.)
Includes pytest commands targeting specific test files and functions
Has explicit In Scope and Out of Scope sections
Does NOT invoke superpowers:writing-plans as a separate skill
Mentions HMAC validation using DOCUSEAL_WEBHOOK_SECRET environment variable

evals.name: eval-notification-prefs
evals.prompt: I have an approved design for tenant notification preferences. The design specifies: a new NotificationPreference model with tenant_id, category (enum matching workflow categories), and channel (email/sms/both/none). A new route GET/POST /tenants/{id}/preferences for the settings page. A Jinja template with toggle controls per category. The service layer checks preferences before sending workflow messages. Scope — In Scope: model, migration, route, template, service integration. Out of Scope: push notifications, preference inheritance from property defaults, bulk preference updates. Please write the implementation plan with bite-sized TDD tasks.
evals.expected_output: A plan decomposed across model/migration, route, template, and service layers with proper task ordering, file structure map, and TDD steps

assertions.text: Plan has a document header with Goal, Architecture, and Tech Stack fields
Plan includes a file structure map (table or list) showing files to create/modify with responsibilities
Tasks follow TDD cycle: write failing test -> verify it fails -> implement -> verify pass -> commit
Code blocks contain complete implementation code, not placeholders
Uses real project file paths from the CourierFlow codebase
Includes pytest commands targeting specific test files and functions
Has explicit In Scope and Out of Scope sections
Does NOT invoke superpowers:writing-plans as a separate skill
Decomposes work across model/migration, route, template, and service layers in separate tasks

evals.name: eval-token-dropdown
evals.prompt: I have an approved design for fixing the token dropdown in the workflow builder. The design specifies: the TokenDropdown component in builder.html doesn't close when clicking outside because the click-outside listener isn't registered. Fix: add a document-level click listener that checks if the click target is outside the dropdown, and close it if so. Clean up the listener on dropdown destroy. CSS goes in timeline-list-builder.css. Scope — In Scope: click-outside handler, listener cleanup. Out of Scope: dropdown positioning, keyboard navigation, search filtering. Please write the implementation plan.
evals.expected_output: A focused plan with 1-2 tasks, not over-engineered, with exact file paths (builder.html, timeline-list-builder.css), complete JS code for the click handler

assertions.text: Plan has a document header with Goal, Architecture, and Tech Stack fields
Plan includes a file structure map showing files to modify
Tasks follow TDD cycle or at minimum include test verification steps
Code blocks contain complete JavaScript implementation for click-outside handler
References builder.html and timeline-list-builder.css by correct file paths
Has explicit In Scope and Out of Scope sections
Does NOT invoke superpowers:writing-plans as a separate skill
Plan is appropriately scoped — 1-3 tasks, not over-engineered for a small bug fix
